target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Channel echo / session pinning with native Telegram echo

This PR lets a session mirror future turns to pinned echo targets, so a webchat,
control UI, CLI, or Telegram turn can be followed from another channel that shares the
same session key.

### Behavior

- User turns are mirrored to echo targets with a source marker such as
  `[via webchat]`, so the destination can tell where the prompt originated.
- Assistant replies are mirrored as native destination-channel messages, not as
  `[echo]`-tagged copies.
- Telegram registers the first native streaming echo renderer. When a Telegram target is
  available, the source run is replayed into Telegram's own draft-stream primitive; when
  streaming is unavailable, the existing post-hoc final mirror remains the fallback.
- A streamed target is filtered out of the post-hoc mirror for that run, and direct
  `chat.send` prompt echo now marks the dispatch context so the generic hook does not
  send a second prompt copy.

### Source and test coverage

The current head is `88943686569ebff43fecc25fc51be6b9bf621e3c`. The Telegram
Desktop proof below exercises the real webchat-to-Telegram delivery path on
`e52925a313521f420f8ee8e0ac9cc6ab8c484a22`. The commits after that proof do
not change Telegram message rendering; they change the SDK/API ownership boundary and
the plugin lifecycle point where Telegram may register the renderer. Those current-head
deltas are covered by the source-level tests listed here.

- `src/infra/outbound/echo.ts` and `src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.ts`: native-final
  assistant mirror options, post-hoc fallback filtering, and duplicate prompt suppression.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`: `chat.send` direct user echo and source-reply
  assistant echo go through the same native-final / streaming-handled gate.
- `src/infra/outbound/echo-mirror-resolver.ts` and `src/infra/outbound/echo-streaming.ts`:
  runId event replay, renderer fan-out, per-run handled marks, and fallback behavior.
- `src/plugins/types.ts`, `src/plugins/api-builder.ts`, and `src/plugins/registry.ts`:
  `api.registerEchoRendererFactory(factory)` is bound by the host to the activated
  plugin's live registered channel id; plugins no longer pass or claim an arbitrary
  channel string.
- `src/plugin-sdk/channel-echo.ts`: the public `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath is
  now types-only, so it no longer exports the global registration function.
- `extensions/telegram/index.ts`, `extensions/telegram/echo-renderer-api.ts`, and
  `extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.ts`: Telegram registers its renderer
  through the owner-scoped full-runtime plugin API. Tool-discovery mode returns before
  the side effect can set the idempotency guard, so a discovery pass cannot suppress the
  later full activation.
- `extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer*.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/client-options.ts`:
  Telegram native renderer registration, draft-stream rendering, and reuse of Telegram
  client options.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`: docs describe the
  owner-scoped activation API and the types-only channel echo subpath.

Commands run locally on the current head after the owner-binding and lifecycle fixes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/index.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.test.ts src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-streaming.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
git diff --check
```

Earlier focused validation for the duplicate-suppression and Telegram-rendering fixes
also passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.proxy.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.send-user-echo.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-mirror-resolver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-streaming.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts
```

Focused Marmorkrebs/Stryker mutation on the source-reply echo block in
`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` killed 14/19 mutants (73.68%). The surviving
mutants were truthy/optional-chain equivalents; the mutants that would regress delivery
options, filtering, payload, and role handling were killed.

### Native Telegram proof - webchat to Telegram

Mantis ran the native Telegram Desktop proof against the rendering head immediately
before the owner-binding SDK/API and lifecycle-guard changes:

- Run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27172400676
- Mantis comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88815#issuecomment-4654447721
- Artifact: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27172400676/artifacts/7494836149
- Baseline: `72f74b33e129233d11fce9d9676464ee009d0f46`, status `pass`
- Candidate: `e52925a313521f420f8ee8e0ac9cc6ab8c484a22`, status `pass`

The Mantis artifact includes before/after Telegram Desktop screenshots, GIFs, MP4 clips,
and JSON summaries. The candidate run started the gateway from that PR head, added a
Telegram echo target with `sessions.echo`, sent a webchat `chat.send` turn, and captured
the resulting Telegram Desktop delivery in the leased test group.

The current head's additional source delta is the registry ownership/lifecycle fix:
`plugin-sdk/channel-echo` no longer exports the registration function, Telegram registers
through the full plugin activation API, and `src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts`
proves the host rejects registration before channel ownership and stores the factory under
the owning plugin/channel id. `extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.test.ts`
also proves that a tool-discovery pass does not consume the later full registration
attempt.

### Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: A webchat `chat.send` turn pinned to a Telegram echo target should
  create a real Telegram-visible echo of the prompt and assistant response while avoiding
  duplicate prompt/final deliveries from the fallback hook paths. A plugin should not be
  able to claim another channel's native echo renderer registry slot.
- Real environment tested: Mantis Telegram Desktop proof workflow on GitHub Actions,
  using an OpenClaw crabbox, a leased test Telegram account/group, baseline
  `72f74b33e129233d11fce9d9676464ee009d0f46`, and candidate
  `e52925a313521f420f8ee8e0ac9cc6ab8c484a22`. Current-head source tests ran locally on
  `88943686569ebff43fecc25fc51be6b9bf621e3c` for the owner-binding registry and
  Telegram lifecycle-guard changes.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: The Mantis workflow resolved the PR head,
  checked out the baseline and candidate refs, started the candidate gateway, added a
  Telegram echo target through `sessions.echo` with `echoUser: true` and
  `echoAssistant: true`, sent `chat.send` with message `Mantis Telegram echo proof:
  mirror this webchat turn.`, then recorded Telegram Desktop screenshot/GIF/MP4 evidence.
  After the owner-binding and lifecycle-guard commits, focused current-head tests
  verified that public SDK registration is gone, Telegram uses the owner-scoped API, the
  registry binds the factory to the owning channel id, and tool-discovery cannot consume
  the later full Telegram renderer registration.
- Evidence after fix: Mantis published visual and metadata evidence for the delivery path:
  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88815#issuecomment-4654447721 and
  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27172400676/artifacts/7494836149.
  Current-head tests additionally covered the SDK ownership and lifecycle fixes:
  `extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.test.ts`,
  `src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts`, and
  `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`.
- Observed result after fix: The Mantis run completed successfully and captured native
  Telegram Desktop proof for the webchat-to-Telegram echo path. Current-head tests confirm
  the public SDK no longer exposes owner-unbound registration and that the plugin registry
  binds renderer registration to the owning channel plugin. They also confirm a
  tool-discovery pass cannot mark Telegram echo registration complete before full
  activation owns the channel.
- What was not tested: Personal daily-use Telegram bot, non-Telegram streaming renderers,
  and final maintainer wording for operator-facing echo target docs.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The visible proof uses a leased Mantis
  Telegram test group with private details redacted by the artifact publisher. The
  screenshot/GIF proves the real Telegram delivery path; source-level tests cover the
  non-visible delivery gates, duplicate-suppression branches, and SDK owner-binding
  boundary.

### Review state

This branch addresses the code findings raised so far:

- source-reply final mirror bypassing the native-final / streaming gate
- duplicate direct `chat.send` prompt echo delivery
- public SDK registration surface allowing non-owning plugins to claim a channel
- Telegram tool-discovery lifecycle suppressing later full-mode echo renderer registration
- Telegram echo renderer failing to reuse configured Telegram client options

Remaining maintainer calls are product/API policy: operator-facing echo target docs,
privacy expectations for persistent cross-transport echo targets, and whether the new
owner-scoped plugin API should remain the stable renderer registration surface.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
